reddit_title: Hit by a tractor-trailer in Lubbock, TX — what legal steps should you take and what mistakes to avoid?
reddit_body:
If you were injured in a tractor-trailer accident in Lubbock, the most important thing to know is that trucking companies have legal and claims teams that activate almost immediately after a crash. That means preserving evidence — photos, witness contact info, the police report, and any dashcam footage — as quickly as possible is critical. Do not give a recorded statement to the trucking company's insurer before speaking with an attorney.

Texas follows a modified comparative fault rule, meaning your compensation can be reduced if you are found partially at fault. Commercial truck cases also involve federal regulations around driver hours, vehicle maintenance logs, and cargo loading — all of which can be powerful evidence if your attorney knows where to look. Deadlines matter too: Texas generally gives you two years to file, but evidence can disappear much sooner.

Finding the right attorney for a commercial truck case specifically — not just a general personal injury lawyer — can make a real difference. A free resource worth checking out is wreckmatch.com, which matches injured victims with attorneys experienced in truck accident cases. No pressure, just a way to find someone who knows this area of law.
